hero.description: Together with WebMCP and Generative UI, forms a LLM-centered trinity web AI architecture enabling a full in-browser intent-to-execute loop.

items.title: LLM
items.desc: Performs semantic reasoning and orchestration, acting as a routing engine that parses user intent and retrieves matching WebSkill documents from the frontend skill library.

items.title: WebSkill
items.desc: Bridges LLM, task execution and UI. Exposes instructions and dependent tools on demand, and strictly defines parameter schemas required.

items.title: Generative UI
items.desc: Intercepts streaming JSON outputs from the LLM based on schema and renders standard web controls (inputs, selects) to ensure accurate parameter collection.

items.title: WebMCP
items.desc: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) tailored for frontend web apps. Operates on current page DOM nodes or sends requests carrying the user's session state.

items.title: Mitigate context explosion
items.desc: WebSkill dynamically loads only the tools required for a matched intent, drastically reducing token usage and improving intent recognition in complex enterprise scenarios.

items.title: Frontend-native closed loop
items.desc: Skill documents live in the browser and tools run client-side, inheriting cookies, localStorage and session state for zero sync cost task execution.

items.title: Agile iteration and self-evolution
items.desc: Skills are lightweight declarative documents editable in visual editors without backend deployment. Agents can observe repeated user flows and solidify them into new WebSkills.

standards.heading: OPFS-based WebSkill Standardization
standards.paragraph: Origin Private File System (OPFS) lets webpages read/write files in an isolated private directory visible only to the current origin.
list: Strict same-origin isolation to prevent cross-origin access to enterprise skill definitions.
Local AES-256-GCM encryption for sensitive business data.
Structured bindings: separate uiSchema from mcpBindings to clarify displayed forms and tooling.
Built-in deep defense: enforce domain allowlists when binding low-level tools to lock data flow.

items.title: Code-level hard boundaries and execution constraints
items.desc: Implement absolute permission blocking in the WebMCP SDK with strict domain whitelists to limit outgoing network requests.

items.title: Human-in-the-loop confirmations
items.desc: For sensitive DOM operations, local file reads or cross-origin calls, require an unskippable native authorization dialog via Generative UI.

items.title: Content boundary marking
items.desc: Wrap untrusted webpage data with explicit delimiters before sending to the LLM to help models distinguish trusted WebSkill instructions from untrusted DOM text.
